title: Kaworu and The Mysterious Stranger
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu and The Mysterious Stranger             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:42 pm Here's an excerpt from a televised adaptation of 'The Mysterious Stranger'. I find the portrayal to be Kaworu-esque. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nUvwbEcKU88 Opinions, etc invited.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:28 pm Relevant: Carl Horn's article on The Mysterious Stranger published in volume 9 of the Evangelion manga, circa 2004: http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_horn04.php

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Tue Feb 18, 2014 5:36 pm Yes, though if I recall correctly from when I read the article, Horn had only been familiar with the forged version of The Mysterious Stranger. Before NGE was created, the 'true' version, which was very different from the prior one in most ways, was discovered and published. (Refer to http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Mysterious_Stranger to brush up on what I'm talking about. I'm reading the true version currently. It's rather close to the film version shown here, which I watched in its entirety, but the book is more thematically illustrative. The distinction is important, as the relationship between Number 44 and August and between Kaoru and Shinji is quite similar. I think whoever invented Kaoru's character would have drawn on that version of TMS, if at all. heck, there was even a cat involved!

Article: Kaworu and The Mysterious Stranger
[]: Kaworu and The Mysterious Stranger             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:42 pm Here's an excerpt from a televised adaptation of 'The Mysterious Stranger'. I find the portrayal to be Kaworu-esque. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nUvwbEcKU88 Opinions, etc invited.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:28 pm Relevant: Carl Horn's article on The Mysterious Stranger published in volume 9 of the Evangelion manga, circa 2004: http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_horn04.php

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Tue Feb 18, 2014 5:36 pm Yes, though if I recall correctly from when I read the article, Horn had only been familiar with the forged version of The Mysterious Stranger. Before NGE was created, the 'true' version, which was very different from the prior one in most ways, was discovered and published. (Refer to http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Mysterious_Stranger to brush up on what I'm talking about. I'm reading the true version currently. It's rather close to the film version shown here, which I watched in its entirety, but the book is more thematically illustrative. The distinction is important, as the relationship between Number 44 and August and between Kaoru and Shinji is quite similar. I think whoever invented Kaoru's character would have drawn on that version of TMS, if at all. heck, there was even a cat involved!
